q: What is defined under Article 1156 of the Civil Code of the Philippines as a 'juridical necessity to give, to do, or not to do'?
The Latin word 'obligare', from which 'obligation' is derived, literally means:
Which source of obligation arises from voluntary, unilateral, and lawful acts, preventing unjust enrichment?
What is a legal wrong, committed through fault or negligence, causing damage to a person or property where no pre-existing contract exists?
An obligation that is demandable at once because its fulfillment does not depend on any future or uncertain event is a:
An obligation whose acquisition of rights depends upon the happening of an event that constitutes the condition is subject to a:
An obligation where the happening of the condition extinguishes or terminates the pre-existing rights is subject to a:
What type of period has a suspensive effect, meaning the obligation becomes demandable only upon the arrival of the specified day?
What type of period has a resolutory effect, meaning the obligation is valid immediately but terminates upon the arrival of the specified day?
Which kind of period is established and mandated directly by law?
Which kind of period is authorized, fixed, or determined by the courts?
A contract where all the essential elements are present, but consent is vitiated by mistake, violence, intimidation, undue influence, or fraud is:
A contract that has no force and effect from the very beginning, as if it had never been entered into, is classified as:
Contracts that cannot be sued upon or enforced in court unless they are ratified because they were entered into without proper authority are:
What type of clause is an accessory undertaking in an obligation to pay a specified penalty in case of breach?
Republic Act No. 9292 is professionally known as the:
a: Electronics and Communications Engineering Law of 2004

q: Under RA 9292, the Professional Regulatory Board of Electronics Engineering consists of a Chairman and how many members?
Who has the executive power to appoint the Chairman and members of the Board of Electronics Engineering under RA 9292?
Which of the following is not a registered category of practice under Republic Act No. 9292?
What is the acronym of the sole Accredited Professional Organization (APO) for electronics practitioners in the Philippines?
Under RA 9292, what registration category is required to sign and seal electronics plans, drawings, and technical specifications?
What is the minimum number of years of active professional practice required for a registered ECE to qualify for upgrade to PECE?
How many recommendations from registered PECEs are required for an ECE applicant to apply for the PECE license under RA 9292?
Which of the following is a key requirement under RA 9292 to qualify for the Electronics Technician (ECT) licensure examination?
a: Completion of at least 1st year of an ECE curriculum
b: Completion of at least 2nd year of an ECE curriculum

q: The Philippine Electronics Code (PEC) governs the technical safety standards for which of the following installations?

What natural hazard is the primary focus of lightning protection standards in the Philippine Electronics Code?
What refers to the physical effects resulting from abnormally high sound levels, which can cause minor discomfort to serious hearing injury?

What organization in the Philippines sets the ethical and operational standards for broadcasting stations and practitioners?

Under KBP Broadcast Standards, what is the maximum commercial load limit for provincial radio stations per hour?
Under KBP Broadcast Standards, what is the maximum commercial load limit for television stations per hour in Metro Manila?
Which of the following practices is strictly prohibited under the KBP Broadcast Code's ethical guidelines?
The KBP Broadcast Code bans the advertisement of which of the following services?
The science dealing with the development and application of devices involving the flow of electrons in a vacuum, gas, or semiconductor is defined under RA 9292 as:
Under the Philippine Electronics Code, what is the recommended minimum overhead clearance for telecommunications lines crossing public streets?
Under the Philippine Electronics Code, what is the minimum clearance required for telecommunications lines from residential windows and fire escapes?
What is the maximum conduit fill factor (%) specified by the Philippine Electronics Code for a conduit containing three or more communications cables?
What is the maximum conduit fill factor (%) specified by the Philippine Electronics Code for a conduit containing exactly two communications cables?
What is the maximum conduit fill factor (%) specified by the Philippine Electronics Code for a conduit containing exactly one communications cable?
In the standard 25-pair color code for telecommunications cables, what is the color of the first binder group (Pair 1-5) Tip conductor?
In the standard 25-pair color code, what is the Ring color of the first pair?
In the standard 25-pair color code, what is the Ring color of the second pair?
In the standard 25-pair color code, what is the Ring color of the third pair?
In the standard 25-pair color code, what is the Ring color of the fourth pair?
In the standard 25-pair color code, what is the Ring color of the fifth pair?
Under KBP Rules, what is the minimum required percentage of local music airplay (OPM) per hour for FM radio stations?
What is the penalty range for first-time major violations of broadcasting ethics under the KBP code?
b: Fines and suspension of broadcast privileges

q: An obligation where only one prestation has been agreed upon, but the obligor may render another in substitution, is a:
An obligation where the debtor is bound to deliver several prestations and is completely released by fulfilling any one of them is a(n):
An obligation where each of the creditors has a right to demand, and each of the debtors is bound to render, compliance with the entire prestation is:
An obligation where the whole liability is distributed among the debtors, and each creditor can only demand his proportionate share of the credit, is:
The neglect or failure to perform an obligation on time, which constitutes a legal delay, is termed:
Legal delay on the part of the debtor to perform his obligation is officially known as:
Legal delay on the part of the creditor to accept the performance of the obligation is officially known as:
The mutual delay or default of both the debtor and the creditor in reciprocal obligations is termed:
An extraordinary event that cannot be foreseen, or which though foreseen is inevitable, exempting the debtor from liability, is a:
A contract that is valid and enforceable but may be set aside by a court due to economic damage or lesion suffered by one of the parties is:
Under the Civil Code, who is liable for damages caused by animals, even if they escape or go astray, unless there is a fortuitous event?
b: The possessor or user of the animal

q: What type of damage is awarded by courts to rebuild, repair, or compensate for the actual pecuniary loss suffered by a party?
What type of damage is awarded to make an example of the wrongdoer and deter others from committing similar acts?
What damages are agreed upon by the parties to a contract, to be paid in case of breach thereof?
What damages may be recovered when the court finds that some pecuniary loss has been suffered but its amount cannot, from the nature of the case, be proved with certainty?

What is the weight (%) allocated to Transmission, Acoustics, and Communications in the ECE Licensure Examination under RA 9292?

What is the minimum required wire size (AWG) for a standard telecommunication grounding electrode conductor?

Under KBP Broadcast Standards, what is the maximum commercial load limit for AM radio stations in Metro Manila per hour?

An ECE licensure candidate receives the following grades: Mathematics = 75%, Electronics = 72%, GEAS = 68%, and Communications = 71%. Calculate his weighted general average to determine if he passed the exam.
An examinee gets: Mathematics = 80%, Electronics = 85%, GEAS = 49%, and Communications = 82%. What is the status of the examinee's licensure outcome?
b: Failed, because GEAS is below the 50% subject threshold
c: Conditioned, needing retake in GEAS only

q: An examinee gets: Mathematics = 52%, Electronics = 71%, GEAS = 72%, and Communications = 75%. Calculate his weighted general average and determine if he passed.
An examinee receives the following scores: Mathematics = 70%, Electronics = 70%, GEAS = 70%, and Communications = 70%. Calculate his weighted general average and status.

Under the Philippine Electronics Code, if a telecom engineer is installing five communications cables of 12 mm outer diameter each inside a conduit, what is the minimum required cross-sectional area of the conduit using the 40% fill factor rule?

Using the conduit sizing requirements from the previous question, determine the closest standard trade size of the conduit required. (Internal area: 1/2 inch = 300 mm^2, 3/4 inch = 500 mm^2, 1 inch = 850 mm^2, 1-1/2 inch = 1900 mm^2)

A telecommunication installation requires running two cables of 15 mm outer diameter each through a PVC conduit. What is the minimum required internal area of the conduit using the 31% fill factor rule for two cables?
Using the conduit area calculation from the previous question, select the appropriate standard conduit trade size. (Internal area: 1 inch = 850 mm^2, 1-1/4 inch = 1350 mm^2, 1-1/2 inch = 1900 mm^2)

A single major fiber optic backbone cable of 20 mm outer diameter must be run through a rigid conduit. What is the minimum required internal area of the conduit using the 53% fill factor rule for a single cable?
Using the single cable conduit area calculation from the previous question, choose the correct trade size. (Internal area: 3/4 inch = 500 mm^2, 1 inch = 850 mm^2, 1-1/4 inch = 1350 mm^2)
